title: Consumer Apps
summary: Consumer products are where privacy stops being an abstract property and becomes part of ordinary user experience.
description: Everyday applications offering private identities and secure interactions.

bodySections.title: Invisible Privacy
bodySections.body: Consumer applications benefit from confidentiality when the protection feels natural instead of ceremonial. Messaging, reading history, local-first communication, or identity-sensitive flows all improve when privacy is built into the system rather than bolted on at the edges.

bodySections.title: Atlas Reading
bodySections.body: This territory broadens the story the atlas can tell. It shows that Arcium is not only for protocol engineers or market operators; it can also sit underneath products that ordinary users interact with every day.
